proven.claim: PROVEN: issueExists returning true implies exact-title match was present in gh results
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 101
; PROVEN: issueExists returning true implies exact-title match was present in gh results
; Pattern: preconditions + transitions + NEGATION of property → unsat
(declare-const matching_in_results Int)  ; count of returned issues with exact title === title
(declare-const return_value Int)         ; 1 = true, 0 = false
; Code transition: existing.some(issue => issue.title === title)
; some() with strict equality returns true iff at least one result has exact match
(assert (= return_value (ite (>= matching_in_results 1) 1 0)))
(assert (>= matching_in_results 0))
; Negation of property: returns true but no exact match was in results
(assert (= return_value 1))
(assert (= matching_in_results 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: --limit 50 truncates results; issue ranked beyond position 50 renders predicate false
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 101
; --limit 50 truncates results; issue ranked beyond position 50 renders predicate false
(declare-const limit Int)             ; hardcoded --limit value in execSync call
(declare-const issue_rank Int)        ; 1-indexed rank of target issue in GitHub search results
(declare-const issues_returned Int)   ; count of issues actually returned by gh
(declare-const target_in_batch Int)   ; 1 = target issue is within returned batch, 0 = absent
(declare-const return_value Int)      ; 1 = true, 0 = false
; Code transition: --limit is hardcoded to 50
(assert (= limit 50))
; Code transition: gh returns at most limit issues
(assert (<= issues_returned limit))
(assert (> issues_returned 0))
; Code transition: target issue exists in GitHub but its rank exceeds the limit
(assert (> issue_rank 0))
(assert (> issue_rank limit))
; Code transition: rank > limit → target not included in returned batch
(assert (= target_in_batch 0))
; Code transition: return = existing.some(...) = 0 when target absent from batch
(assert (= return_value target_in_batch))
; Violation: predicate returns false despite issue existing at rank 51+
(assert (= return_value 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: catch block returns false on any gh/network/timeout error, misreporting nonexistence
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 101
; catch block returns false on any gh/network/timeout error, misreporting nonexistence
(declare-const gh_succeeds Int)   ; 1 = execSync completes, 0 = throws (timeout/error)
(declare-const issue_exists Int)  ; 1 = issue actually exists on GitHub
(declare-const return_value Int)  ; 1 = true, 0 = false
; Code transition: execSync throws on timeout (15000 ms limit) or gh CLI error
(assert (= gh_succeeds 0))
; Code transition: catch block unconditionally returns false — no rethrow, no signal
(assert (=> (= gh_succeeds 0) (= return_value 0)))
; Issue exists in GitHub (independent of whether gh could reach it)
(assert (= issue_exists 1))
; Violation: predicate reports nonexistence under error; caller files a duplicate
(assert (= return_value 0))
(assert (= issue_exists 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: gh --search is full-text ranked search, not an exact-title equality filter;
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 101
; gh --search is full-text ranked search, not an exact-title equality filter;
; an existing issue may not be returned even within the result window
(declare-const issue_exists_in_github Int)   ; 1 = issue with exact title exists
(declare-const search_surfaces_target Int)   ; 1 = full-text search returns target in batch
(declare-const target_in_results Int)        ; 1 = target present in parsed JSON
(declare-const return_value Int)             ; 1 = true, 0 = false
; Code transition: issue exists; title is long and punctuation-heavy (brackets, colons, dashes)
(assert (= issue_exists_in_github 1))
; Code transition: --search uses ranked full-text search; target may not score into top 50
(assert (= search_surfaces_target 0))
; Code transition: if search does not return target, it is absent from JSON output
(assert (= target_in_results search_surfaces_target))
; Code transition: existing.some(...) returns false when target absent
(assert (= return_value target_in_results))
; Violation: predicate returns false — issue exists but search engine missed it
(assert (= return_value 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Information flow: title containing shell metacharacters flows through JSON.stringify
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 101
; Information flow: title containing shell metacharacters flows through JSON.stringify
; into a shell-executed string where $, `, \ are still interpreted by /bin/sh
(declare-const title_has_shell_meta Int)       ; 1 = title contains $, backtick, or \
(declare-const json_stringify_escapes_meta Int) ; 1 = JSON.stringify neutralises metachar
(declare-const shell_interprets_meta Int)       ; 1 = /bin/sh evaluates the metacharacter
(declare-const unintended_command_runs Int)     ; 1 = arbitrary shell expression executes
; Code transition: JSON.stringify escapes " and \ but NOT $ or backticks for shell context
(assert (= title_has_shell_meta 1))
(assert (= json_stringify_escapes_meta 0))      ; $ and ` pass through unescaped
; Code transition: execSync shells out via /bin/sh -c; double-quoted $ is still expanded
(assert (=> (= json_stringify_escapes_meta 0) (= shell_interprets_meta title_has_shell_meta)))
; Code transition: shell interpretation of injected token executes its content
(assert (=> (= shell_interprets_meta 1) (= unintended_command_runs 1)))
; Violation: unintended command executes when title contains metacharacters
(assert (= unintended_command_runs 1))
(check-sat)
